methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $36M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Infrastructure & Logistics (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Infrastructure & Logistics, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Earth AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Infrastructure & Logistics), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Amperon is a Series B infrastructure and logistics company that has raised $36M to date. The private peer set in this sector is sparse, comprising only two companies (AiDash and Earth AI); no public comparables exist in the immediate sector, limiting the breadth of valuation triangulation.

Given the limited peer universe, stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method. Using total capital raised ($36M) as a proxy for post-Series A valuation, a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B to next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $68M. This multiplier reflects observed progression patterns across the broader venture stage continuum, though it carries model risk given Amperon's discrete sector positioning.

The peer-median approach is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables meeting minimum cohort size (three datapoints). Relying on an all-sector median would introduce excessive noise given the specialized nature of infrastructure and logistics venture investments. The analysis therefore triangulates to a range of $58M (low), $68M (median), and $79M (high), with the median representing the sole quantitative method output. Key uncertainties include capital efficiency relative to the sparse peer set, unit economics in a capital-intensive sector, and the absence of public market signals for validation. The range should be treated as preliminary pending access to expanded private market data or near-term revenue scaling milestones.
